transcript.text: I'm an automated medical assistant. If this is an emergency, hang up and call 911. Do you consent to continue? (yes/no)
Yes.
What is your main medical concern today?
Well, I've been having some constipation issues.
When did your constipation start?
It's started maybe, like, 3 weeks ago.
Any associated symptoms like abdominal pain, blood in stool, or weight loss?
No.
Have you noticed any changes in your bowel habits, such as stool caliber, frequency, or difficulty passing stool?
No. No.
Have you tried any treatments or remedies for your constipation so far?

I've tried the Murallax. I have tried some other over the counter other ones. I've tried more fiber in my diet. I've tried drinking more water. I've tried a lot of things, but nothing seems to be helping.
Any history of recent travel, new medications, or illnesses before the constipation started?
No.
Any history of abdominal surgeries, neurological conditions, or metabolic disorders like diabetes?
No. No.
Any recent unexplained fatigue, night sweats, or fevers?
No.
Have you noticed any changes in appetite or any nausea or vomiting?
No. No. No vomiting. But I am a little bit more nauseous every now and then, but my appetite is the same except when I'm nauseous, of course.
Have you noticed any abdominal bloating or distension?
Yes. I have. I am very bloated. My abdomen is huge. And that's not getting any better no matter how many crunches I do.
Have you noticed any vomiting, especially if it is persistent or projectile?
No. No vomiting, period.
The next few questions may feel repetitive, but they are important for medication safety and treatment decisions. How old are you? Please say your age in years.
Do you have any medication allergies? If yes, list them. If no, say no.
Penicillin.
What sex were you assigned at birth? Please say male or female.
Male.
Have you ever been told you have kidney disease or significantly reduced kidney function? (yes/no/not sure)
No.
Have you ever been told you have liver disease (such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver, or abnormal liver tests)? (yes/no/not sure)
No.

It sounds like you’ve been dealing with constipation and bloating for a few weeks now. Based on what you’ve told me, this is most likely chronic functional constipation. Because this is a phone visit, I can’t examine you or run tests, but a licensed physician will review your information and may recommend medication like a gentle laxative to help. In the meantime, you can continue to manage symptoms with fiber, fluids, and over-the-counter options if safe for you. If you develop severe belly pain, vomiting, or can’t pass stool or gas, please seek urgent in-person care right away. Otherwise, if symptoms persist or worsen, consider seeing a healthcare provider for further evaluation. A physician will review your case and decide on the best next steps.
